DEC-002.title: Approve PMO scoring framework
DEC-002.objective: Enable consistent, repeatable ranking of ready-for-agent GitHub issues before autonomous dispatch consumes the weekly budget.
DEC-002.decision: Approve, modify, or defer the PMO scoring framework.
mece: Strategic value, unblocker effect, revenue adjacency
13 issues waiting; no agreed rank order
Agent complexity, external writes, rollback cost
Tier-B proposal exists; scores not standardized
Budget fit ($20/wk), trust tier, Nick-only gates
DEC-002 is the last high-priority framework gate
DEC-002.root_cause: PMO cannot scale triage without a shared rubric — ad-hoc ranking would misallocate the $20/week cap across low-ROI agent runs.

options.name: Approve default framework (pilot)
options.upside: Unblocks PMO-001 immediately; impact×readiness/effort is good enough for first cycle.
options.downside: Weights may need tuning after first spend report.
options.when: Nick wants velocity over perfect calibration.

options.name: Approve with Nick-tuned weights
options.upside: Better fit to Nick's ROI preferences before any spend.
options.downside: Adds one review round; delays first dispatch.
options.when: Nick has strong priors on issue priority.

options.name: Defer — manual CEO triage only
options.downside: Does not scale; wastes PMO capacity; blocks autonomous loop.
options.when: Only if autonomous dispatch is off indefinitely.

options.name: Reject — revert to ad-hoc picks
options.downside: Highest risk of budget waste and inconsistent outcomes.
options.when: Not recommended while budget is capped and worker may enable.

DEC-002.recommendation: **Approve the default framework for a pilot on the top 3 issues.** Revisit weights after the first weekly spend report (CFO).
DEC-002.what_nick_must_do: Reply approve / approve-with-weights / defer. CEO appends `decision_resolved` for DEC-002.

DEC-003.title: Confirm Telegram alert method for $5 spend thresholds
DEC-003.objective: Give Nicholas real-time visibility when weekly OpenRouter spend crosses material thresholds — before the $20 cap is silently exhausted.
DEC-003.decision: Which alert channel and threshold rules to use for spend notifications.
mece: Proposed: every $5 of cumulative weekly spend
DEC-003.root_cause: Budget is authorized and capped, but spend is only visible on reconcile — no interrupt path for Nick to throttle or reprioritize mid-week.

options.name: Telegram bot → Nick
options.upside: Real-time; mobile; low friction once bot token is set.
options.downside: Requires one-time bot setup and chat ID.

options.name: Dashboard + reconcile only
options.downside: No proactive signal; Nick must pull.
options.when: Acceptable only at very low spend velocity.

options.name: Hard stop at $18 auto-pause
options.downside: No human judgment before pause; may halt good work.
options.when: Combine with A or B, not alone.

DEC-003.recommendation: **Telegram alerts at $5 / $10 / $15 / $20 cumulative weekly spend**, with reconcile as fallback witness. Pair with auto-pause at $18 if desired.
DEC-003.what_nick_must_do: Confirm Telegram (provide chat ID or approve CTO to wire bot) or choose email/dashboard-only.

ISSUE-24.title: DECISION: revisit Telegram bot permission posture
ISSUE-24.objective: Decide how much the Telegram PA (@NMGs_Hermes_bot) and the agent fleet may act on Nicks behalf over Telegram, since the bridge can already run Claude Code with email/calendar/contacts tools.
ISSUE-24.decision: Choose the Telegram PA permission posture: keep current, tighten, or expand.
mece: Which tools the bridge may invoke (read vs send email, calendar edit, shell/Claude Code)
Bridge currently can run Claude Code + Gmail modify + calendar edit
Who may message the bot / allowlist
Outward-facing or irreversible actions (send email, external posts)
No explicit per-action confirm policy documented
ISSUE-24.root_cause: The PA accreted capabilities (email/calendar/Claude Code) without an explicit decision on what it may do autonomously vs with confirmation — so it sits as a standing Nick gate.

options.name: Keep current posture (Nick-only allowlist, full tools)
options.upside: No work; PA stays maximally useful.
options.downside: No confirmation gate on outward/irreversible actions.
options.when: Nick trusts the single-user allowlist as sufficient control.

options.name: Tighten — read/draft only, confirm before send
options.upside: Removes risk of autonomous outward actions.
options.downside: Less hands-free; adds a confirm step.
options.when: Nick wants the PA to never send/act without an explicit yes.

options.name: Expand — add scheduling/contacts write, more autonomy
options.when: Nick wants a fuller autonomous assistant.

ISSUE-24.recommendation: **Tighten to read/draft-by-default with confirm-before-send for outward/irreversible actions**, keeping the Nick-only allowlist. Matches the unbrowse/agent guardrail posture already in memory.
ISSUE-24.what_nick_must_do: Reply keep / tighten / expand (or custom). CEO appends decision_resolved for ISSUE-24.

ISSUE-15.title: FEATURE: optional skill usefulness tiers
ISSUE-15.objective: Let agents/the skill-router know which skills are worth invoking, so the most useful skills surface first instead of all skills being treated equally.
ISSUE-15.decision: Pick the tier schema before agents implement: simple 3-tier label, numeric usefulness score, or defer.
mece: Who sets the tier (Nick, usage stats, or LLM judge)
Undecided — drives whether it is manual or auto
How the router uses tiers (filter vs rank)
skill-router exists; no tier input today
Keeping tiers current as skills change
Manual tiers go stale; auto needs telemetry
ISSUE-15.root_cause: Skill library has grown large; without a usefulness signal the router cannot prioritise, so this sits as a standing design decision Nick must make before any agent codes it.

options.name: Option A — simple 3-tier label (core / useful / experimental), Nick-set
options.upside: Trivial to implement; full Nick control; no telemetry needed.
options.when: Nick wants a quick, controllable signal now.

options.name: Option B — numeric usefulness score, usage/LLM-derived
options.downside: Needs usage telemetry + scoring loop; more build.
options.when: Nick wants it to self-tune over time.

options.name: Defer — no tiers yet
options.downside: Router keeps treating all skills equally.
options.when: Skill count is not yet a real problem.

ISSUE-15.recommendation: **Option A (3-tier, Nick-set) as a pilot** — lowest cost, immediate control; revisit Option B once there is real skill-usage telemetry to drive a score.
ISSUE-15.what_nick_must_do: Reply A / B / defer (and the tier labels if A). CEO appends decision_resolved for ISSUE-15.
